content: Remote OK Video Podcast Editor Betches Media <strong>About Us<br><br></strong>Betches Media is the ultimate digital media and lifestyle destination for women. As a pioneer in the humor and entertainment space, weâve built a powerful platform that connects with over 250M fans across social media, podcasts, web, events, and e-commerce. Our funny, honest, and candid approach resonates with women globally, creating a one-of-a-kind community.<br><br>Now as a part of LBG Media, Betches is in an exciting new chapter, leveraging the global reach and innovation of our parent company to push the boundaries of creative media.<br><br>This is your opportunity to be at the forefront of shaping the future of Betches Media and propel the brand through its next phase of growth.<br><br><strong>Description<br><br></strong>Do you want to put your vast knowledge of random pop culture and online trends to use? Do you wish your endless TikTok scrolling counted as âresearchâ? Would you rather edit a video than write a cover letter? Then you may be perfect for this role. Betches Media is looking for a Video Editor to add to our team!<br><br>The ideal candidate is a highly skilled video podcast editor who is proficient in editing both long-form and short-form podcast content across Betches Media platforms via Spotify, YouTube, Instagram &amp; TikTok. You will play a major part in the post production of short form social media edits and longform multicam podcast edits, as well as the post production of podcast audio.<br><br>Our video podcast editors will work hand-in-hand with producers to develop dynamic content with an eye towards social, and should be excited to infuse their edits with as much creativity as possible. They also should be knowledgeable about the video podcast space, and what works across video platforms. Our editors will also work closely with our social and motion graphics team to ensure our end product is both high-quality and primed for virality.<br><br>This role will report into the Associate Director of Production.<br><br><strong>Roles &amp; Responsibilities<br><br></strong><ul><li>Edit longform video podcasts under tight deadlines, using impeccable creative instincts and industry best practices. Apply revisions and work closely with the Producer to apply episode notes.</li><li>Work closely with the Producer to prep video podcasts episodes to be published on podcasting platforms.</li><li>Edit a variety of social clips for distribution across multiple platforms such as Instagram, TikTok, and Youtube Shorts.</li><li>Focus on maximizing the reach and engagement of your shows, working closely with producers to select and create the most engaging social clips possible.</li><li>Drive all aspects of the video post-production process from ingesting footage, cleaning up audio, color correction, to exporting for final delivery.</li><li>Edit multiple videos lengths and sizes from end-to-end and format according to asset needs.</li><li>Work closely with the Associate Director and Senior Editor to prioritize workflows, follow the post-production calendar closely, and keep projects on schedule.</li><li>Work closely with a Producer on a number of projects to align on editorial and creative output of projects.<br><br></li></ul><strong>Qualifications<br><br></strong><ul><li>Experience editing short-form social videos, interviews, and long-form content.</li><li>Proficiency in Adobe Suite (Premiere, Photoshop, After Effects, etc.).</li><li>Strong understanding of visual storytelling, composition, and typography.</li><li>Ability to organize footage, execute final cuts, and manage post-production.</li><li>Knowledge of key framing, multicam editing, and audio post-production.</li><li>Experience working within brand guidelines to ensure consistency.</li><li>Strong communication, collaboration, and multitasking skills.</li><li>Ability to stay ahead of social media trends and bring new ideas.</li><li>Previous experience in video editing, media, or graphic design is a plus.</li><li>After Effects and audio engineering experience is a plus.</li><li>Comedic background or interest in comedy is a plus. <br><br></li></ul><strong>Compensation<br><br></strong>The expected hourly rate for this role is between $40 - $45, and a commitment of up to 20 hours per week.<br><br><strong>Our DEI+ Commitment<br><br></strong>At Betches, we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace that empowers talent from many different backgrounds, experiences, and identities. We believe that true creativity and knowledge emerge when diversity and inclusion are fully embraced. We strive to create a workforce that reflects the diversity of our clients and our communities, recognizing that our journey towards excellence is ongoing. Together, we aim to foster an environment where everyone can contribute to and benefit from our shared success. design, marketing, exec New York, New York, New York, United States 2026-07-15T03:24:09+00:00 1134873 https://remoteOK.com/remote-jobs/remote-video-podcast-editor-betches-media-1134873 https://remoteOK.com/assets/logo-square.png Chief Growth Officer Original Peptide <p><strong>Company Description</strong> Original Peptide supplies high-quality peptides designed to support strength, energy, recovery, cognitive optimization, and overall wellness research. The company emphasizes rigorous standards of quality, purity, and accessibility, offering trusted compounds to individuals pursuing advanced health and performance goals. Its product range spans muscle support, metabolic balance, and restorative wellness applications, enabling customers to explore innovative approaches to well-being. Original Peptide is committed to providing reliable, cutting-edge tools backed by careful formulation and a strong focus on customer care.</p><p><strong>Role Description</strong> The Chief Growth Officer (CGO) is a full-time, remote role responsible for driving revenue growth, market expansion, and brand visibility for Original Peptide. This role oversees integrated growth strategies across sales, business development, and marketing, ensuring alignment with company objectives and regulatory considerations in the wellness and research sectors. Day-to-day responsibilities include developing and executing growth plans, optimizing customer acquisition and retention, managing sales pipelines, and identifying new market opportunities and strategic partnerships. The CGO will collaborate closely with leadership to set performance targets, analyze data and KPIs, refine pricing and positioning, and guide cross-functional teams toward sustainable, scalable growth. The role also involves staying informed about industry trends, competitive dynamics, and customer needs to inform product and go-to-market strategies.</p><p><strong>Qualifications</strong></p><ul><li>Demonstrated expertise in Sales and Sales Management, including building high-performing teams and managing end-to-end sales cycles.</li><li>Strong Business Development skills, with experience identifying, negotiating, and managing strategic partnerships and new market opportunities.</li><li>Proficiency in Marketing and Digital Marketing, including campaign strategy, performance measurement, and brand positioning across channels.</li><li>Proven ability to develop and execute data-driven growth stra
